lines.text: (reading) [deliberate] Flight Crew Medical Examination. Standards of 1968.
(then) NASA wrote a physical in 1968. [pause] It is the last standard that ever mattered, and it is the one in this binder. We do all of it or none of it.
(far off) Bottom line's got a print date on it. You want that too?
[flatly] Line eleven. Nobody reads line eleven.
There were waivers in '68 too. Compensating technique, documented.
Two minutes after all that stepping. Fifty-four. [pause] Sitting still he's down to fifty-one.
(then) Mine's higher than that right now, just kneeling here. Four hundred dollars, this thing.
There's a knothole in this bench. I'll have it plugged before you're done.
[flatly] Set it for the hour. Don't lift the lid early on my account.
[flatly] Slower on the step box. All four of you. I wrote it down.
